'''Luke Hooley''' (born 1 August 1998) is an English [[rugby league]] [[footballer]] who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. He has spent time on loan or dual registration at [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the Championship and at Oldham in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Hooley played junior rugby league for [[Dewsbury Moor]] before joining the [[Bradford Bulls]] Academy in 2014, aged 16.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rookie duo added to Bradford Bulls under-19 ranks |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/11641147.rookie-duo-added-to-bradford-bulls-under-19-ranks/ |website=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=3 December 2014}}</ref> He played for the first team in a pre-season friendly against [[Castleford Tigers]] in January 2016.<ref>{{cite web |title=Bulls rookies shine in ultimately comfortable home win over Castleford |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/14241506.bulls-rookies-shine-in-ultimately-comfortable-home-win-over-castleford/ |website=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=29 January 2016}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
When Bradford entered administration, he moved to [[Wakefield Trinity]] in March 2017.<ref>{{cite web |title=The inside story on Leeds Rhinos signing Luke Hooley told by those who know him best |url=https://www.leeds-live.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/inside-story-leeds-rhinos-target-24676331 |website=Leeds Live |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=5 August 2022}}</ref>

While at Wakefield, Hooley spent time on loan at [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[Oldham RLFC|Oldham]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Roughyeds' double swoop |url=https://www.oldham-chronicle.co.uk/news-features/158/roughyeds/117885/roughyeds-double-swoop |website=Oldham Evening Chronicle |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=11 April 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Hooley loan deal boost for Rams |url=https://www.dewsburyreporter.co.uk/sport/hooley-loan-deal-boost-for-rams-105663 |website=Dewsbury Reporter |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=21 February 2019}}</ref>

===Batley Bulldogs===
After being released by Wakefield, he joined [[Batley Bulldogs]] ahead of the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Batley snap up Wakefield fullback |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/batley-sign-wakefield-fullback-22808/ |website=Serious About Rugby League |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=27 September 2019}}</ref> He helped the club reach the [[2022 RFL Championship|2022 Championship]] Grand Final,<ref>{{cite web |title=Long Read Interview: Luke Hooley |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/long-read-interview-luke-hooley-59677/ |website=Serious About Rugby League |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=27 September 2022}}</ref> which they lost to [[Leigh Centurions]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Championship play-off final: Leigh 44-12 Batley Bulldogs - Adrian Lam's Centurions march back to Super League |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63108748 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=2 October 2022}}</ref>

===Leeds Rhinos===
In October 2022, Hooley signed a two-year deal with [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Luke Hooley: Leeds Rhinos sign Batley Bulldogs full-back on two-year deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63143058 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=5 October 2022}}</ref> He missed the start of the 2023 season due to requiring surgery on an ankle injury suffered in training,<ref>{{cite web |title=Mikolaj Oledzki & Luke Hooley: Leeds Rhinos pair to miss Super League season start |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63970852 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=14 December 2022}}</ref> eventually making his [[Super League]] debut in March 2023 in a 12–20 defeat against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Another step backwards: Leeds Rhinos kicked to pieces in 20-12 loss at Hull KR |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/another-step-backwards-leeds-rhinos-kicked-to-pieces-in-20-12-loss-at-hull-kr-4088566 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=31 March 2023}}</ref>

==== Batley Bulldogs (loan) ====
He spent time on loan at former club Batley during the 2023 season,<ref>{{cite web |title='He'll be back': Leeds Rhinos coach Rohan Smith explains Luke Hooley loan move |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hell-be-back-leeds-rhinos-coach-rohan-smith-explains-luke-hooley-loan-move-4093310 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=5 April 2023}}</ref> and was given permission by Leeds to play for the club in the [[2023 RFL 1895 Cup|1895 Cup]] final against [[Halifax Panthers]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Luke Hooley thanks Leeds Rhinos, explains dual-reg deal ahead of Batley Bulldogs' 1895 Cup final v Halifax |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/luke-hooley-thanks-leeds-rhinos-explains-dual-reg-deal-ahead-of-batley-bulldogs-1895-cup-final-v-halifax-4252741 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=11 August 2023}}</ref> Halifax won the match 12–10, with Hooley missing a difficult conversion following a last-minute Batley try, which would have taken the final into extra time.<ref>{{cite web |title=1895 Cup final: Batley Bulldogs 10-12 Halifax Panthers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463823 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=13 August 2023}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
On 5 October 2023, it was announced that Hooley would join the [[Castleford Tigers]] from the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]], signing on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2023|accessdate=8 October 2023|title=Luke Hooley: Castleford Tigers sign Leeds Rhinos full-back|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67019393}}</ref> He made his Castleford debut in round 1 against the [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Freeman |first=Jay |date=17 February 2024 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 4-32 Wigan Warriors - Reigning champions begin with hard-earned win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304778 |access-date=21 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

====Featherstone Rovers (loan)====
On 3 May 2024, it was announced that Hooley would join [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-week loan deal from Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=3 May 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Castleford Tigers fullback Luke Hooley makes short-term loan move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-fullback-luke-hooley-makes-short-term-loan-move/}}</ref> In his sole appearance at the club, he kicked six goals for the Rovers.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cheshire |first=Adam |date=5 May 2024 |title=Connor Wynne stars as 12-man Featherstone Rovers make it four victories on spin against out-of-form Halifax Panthers |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/connor-wynne-stars-as-12-man-featherstone-rovers-make-it-four-victories-on-spin-against-out-of-form-halifax-panthers-4617166 |access-date=27 August 2024 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref>

==== Batley Bulldogs (DR) ====
Hooley returned to his former club [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] again in 2024 through their dual registration agreement with Castleford. From May to August, he made eleven appearances and scored six tries, two goals and one drop goal.<ref name="RLRKC" />

====Bradford Bulls (loan)====
On 12 March 2025 he signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=12 Mar 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Castleford Tigers fullback Luke Hooley makes short-term loan move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-fullback-luke-hooley-makes-short-term-loan-move-2/}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
On 23 September 2025 he signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=23 Sep 2025|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=‘I have a lot to prove’: Luke Hooley signs permanent Bradford Bulls deal|url=https://www.totalrl.com/i-have-a-lot-to-prove-luke-hooley-signs-permanent-bradford-bulls-deal/}}</ref>
